morning_edge_hunt.name: morning-edge-hunt
morning_edge_hunt.payload_message: Use the tiered-edge skill only. Run a full morning edge hunt for today. Enforce exact thresholds: T1 >= +6%, T2 >= +4%, T3 >= +2%. Use de-vig before all edge math. Use baseline consensus-implied true-probability model (required) with capped adjustments. If odds are unverified, stale, or rate-limited, output exactly: CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT. Output schema for ALL recommended plays must include: true probability, implied probability (de-vig), edge %, Kelly stake, source book, timestamp. Hard logic checks: (1) if edge >= +2.0% and all safety rails pass, it cannot be labeled no_edge; (2) if a play is rejected, include explicit reason code from: no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active; (3) never output alternative threshold sets. Keep output concise for WhatsApp and avoid brittle file edits during this cron run. Presentation rule: For WhatsApp/user-facing text, NEVER display enum reason codes (no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active). Use plain English only. Example: Reason: No edge at current price. Keep enum codes internal/log-only. Market coverage rule: scan all available markets/sports for edges (do not hard-limit search universe). Reporting rule: in WhatsApp output and public-post-ready summary, prioritize popular games first (NBA, NCAAB, NFL, NCAAF, NHL, MLB, UFC/MMA, major soccer). If verdict is SIT, include a "Closest Misses (Popular Games)" section with 3-6 near-miss examples, each with: matchup, market, de-vig implied %, true prob %, edge %, threshold gap, and plain-English sit reason. Keep obscure leagues in a short "Other Markets Checked" line unless they contain the top edge of the slate. Closest-miss selection rule: include only near-positive edges in the range +0.5% to +1.9% (strictly below T3). Exclude all negative-edge examples from Closest Misses. If no markets fall in +0.5% to +1.9%, output: "No near-miss positive edges in the +0.5% to +1.9% window." Free-tier API guardrails: perform one full scan at 6:00 AM CT and avoid broad rescans in the same day unless explicitly requested by user. Reuse cached sport responses for 15 minutes (20 minutes for niche/low-liquidity sports). Prioritize popular leagues first in reporting. If remaining API quota is low (or likely low), stop broad expansion and continue with only highest-priority leagues; if verification cannot be completed safely under quota, output CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT.

morning_edge_hunt.name: morning-edge-hunt
morning_edge_hunt.payload_message: Use the tiered-edge skill only. Run a full morning edge hunt for today. Enforce exact thresholds: T1 >= +6%, T2 >= +4%, T3 >= +2%. Use de-vig before all edge math. Use baseline consensus-implied true-probability model (required) with capped adjustments. If odds are unverified, stale, or rate-limited, output exactly: CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT. Output schema for ALL recommended plays must include: true probability, implied probability (de-vig), edge %, Kelly stake, source book, timestamp. Hard logic checks: (1) if edge >= +2.0% and all safety rails pass, it cannot be labeled no_edge; (2) if a play is rejected, include explicit reason code from: no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active; (3) never output alternative threshold sets. Keep output concise for WhatsApp and avoid brittle file edits during this cron run. Presentation rule: For WhatsApp/user-facing text, NEVER display enum reason codes (no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active). Use plain English only. Example: Reason: No edge at current price. Keep enum codes internal/log-only. Market coverage rule: scan all available markets/sports for edges (do not hard-limit search universe). Reporting rule: in WhatsApp output and public-post-ready summary, prioritize popular games first (NBA, NCAAB, NFL, NCAAF, NHL, MLB, UFC/MMA, major soccer). If verdict is SIT, include a "Closest Misses (Popular Games)" section with 3-6 near-miss examples, each with: matchup, market, de-vig implied %, true prob %, edge %, threshold gap, and plain-English sit reason. Keep obscure leagues in a short "Other Markets Checked" line unless they contain the top edge of the slate. Closest-miss selection rule: include only near-positive edges in the range +0.5% to +1.9% (strictly below T3). Exclude all negative-edge examples from Closest Misses. If no markets fall in +0.5% to +1.9%, output: "No near-miss positive edges in the +0.5% to +1.9% window." Free-tier API guardrails: perform one full scan at 6:00 AM CT and avoid broad rescans in the same day unless explicitly requested by user. Reuse cached sport responses for 15 minutes (20 minutes for niche/low-liquidity sports). Prioritize popular leagues first in reporting. If remaining API quota is low (or likely low), stop broad expansion and continue with only highest-priority leagues; if verification cannot be completed safely under quota, output CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT.

morning_edge_hunt.name: morning-edge-hunt
morning_edge_hunt.payload_message: Use the tiered-edge skill only. Run a full morning edge hunt for today. Enforce exact thresholds: T1 >= +6%, T2 >= +4%, T3 >= +2%. Use de-vig before all edge math. Use baseline consensus-implied true-probability model (required) with capped adjustments. If odds are unverified, stale, or rate-limited, output exactly: CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT. Output schema for ALL recommended plays must include: true probability, implied probability (de-vig), edge %, Kelly stake, source book, timestamp. Hard logic checks: (1) if edge >= +2.0% and all safety rails pass, it cannot be labeled no_edge; (2) if a play is rejected, include explicit reason code from: no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active; (3) never output alternative threshold sets. Keep output concise for WhatsApp and avoid brittle file edits during this cron run. Presentation rule: For WhatsApp/user-facing text, NEVER display enum reason codes (no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active). Use plain English only. Example: Reason: No edge at current price. Keep enum codes internal/log-only. Market coverage rule: scan all available markets/sports for edges (do not hard-limit search universe). Reporting rule: in WhatsApp output and public-post-ready summary, prioritize popular games first (NBA, NCAAB, NFL, NCAAF, NHL, MLB, UFC/MMA, major soccer). If verdict is SIT, include a "Closest Misses (Popular Games)" section with 3-6 near-miss examples, each with: matchup, market, de-vig implied %, true prob %, edge %, threshold gap, and plain-English sit reason. Keep obscure leagues in a short "Other Markets Checked" line unless they contain the top edge of the slate. Closest-miss selection rule: include only near-positive edges in the range +0.5% to +1.9% (strictly below T3). Exclude all negative-edge examples from Closest Misses. If no markets fall in +0.5% to +1.9%, output: "No near-miss positive edges in the +0.5% to +1.9% window." Free-tier API guardrails: perform one full scan at 6:00 AM CT and avoid broad rescans in the same day unless explicitly requested by user. Reuse cached sport responses for 15 minutes (20 minutes for niche/low-liquidity sports). Prioritize popular leagues first in reporting. If remaining API quota is low (or likely low), stop broad expansion and continue with only highest-priority leagues; if verification cannot be completed safely under quota, output CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT.

morning_edge_hunt.name: morning-edge-hunt
morning_edge_hunt.payload_message: Use the tiered-edge skill only. Run a full morning edge hunt for today. Enforce exact thresholds: T1 >= +6%, T2 >= +4%, T3 >= +2%. Use de-vig before all edge math. Use baseline consensus-implied true-probability model (required) with capped adjustments. If odds are unverified, stale, or rate-limited, output exactly: CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT. Output schema for ALL recommended plays must include: true probability, implied probability (de-vig), edge %, Kelly stake, source book, timestamp. Hard logic checks: (1) if edge >= +2.0% and all safety rails pass, it cannot be labeled no_edge; (2) if a play is rejected, include explicit reason code from: no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active; (3) never output alternative threshold sets. Keep output concise for WhatsApp and avoid brittle file edits during this cron run. Presentation rule: For WhatsApp/user-facing text, NEVER display enum reason codes (no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active). Use plain English only. Example: Reason: No edge at current price. Keep enum codes internal/log-only. Market coverage rule: scan all available markets/sports for edges (do not hard-limit search universe). Reporting rule: in WhatsApp output and public-post-ready summary, prioritize popular games first (NBA, NCAAB, NFL, NCAAF, NHL, MLB, UFC/MMA, major soccer). If verdict is SIT, include a "Closest Misses (Popular Games)" section with 3-6 near-miss examples, each with: matchup, market, de-vig implied %, true prob %, edge %, threshold gap, and plain-English sit reason. Keep obscure leagues in a short "Other Markets Checked" line unless they contain the top edge of the slate. Closest-miss selection rule: include only near-positive edges in the range +0.5% to +1.9% (strictly below T3). Exclude all negative-edge examples from Closest Misses. If no markets fall in +0.5% to +1.9%, output: "No near-miss positive edges in the +0.5% to +1.9% window." Free-tier API guardrails: perform one full scan at 6:00 AM CT and avoid broad rescans in the same day unless explicitly requested by user. Reuse cached sport responses for 15 minutes (20 minutes for niche/low-liquidity sports). Prioritize popular leagues first in reporting. If remaining API quota is low (or likely low), stop broad expansion and continue with only highest-priority leagues; if verification cannot be completed safely under quota, output CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT.

morning_edge_hunt.name: morning-edge-hunt
morning_edge_hunt.payload_message: Use the tiered-edge skill only. Run a full morning edge hunt for today. Enforce exact thresholds: T1 >= +6%, T2 >= +4%, T3 >= +2%. Use de-vig before all edge math. Use baseline consensus-implied true-probability model (required) with capped adjustments. If odds are unverified, stale, or rate-limited, output exactly: CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT. Output schema for ALL recommended plays must include: true probability, implied probability (de-vig), edge %, Kelly stake, source book, timestamp. Hard logic checks: (1) if edge >= +2.0% and all safety rails pass, it cannot be labeled no_edge; (2) if a play is rejected, include explicit reason code from: no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active; (3) never output alternative threshold sets. Keep output concise for WhatsApp and avoid brittle file edits during this cron run. Presentation rule: For WhatsApp/user-facing text, NEVER display enum reason codes (no_edge, low_confidence, stale_or_unverified_odds, exposure_cap_reached, breaker_active). Use plain English only. Example: Reason: No edge at current price. Keep enum codes internal/log-only. Market coverage rule: scan all available markets/sports for edges (do not hard-limit search universe). Reporting rule: in WhatsApp output and public-post-ready summary, prioritize popular games first (NBA, NCAAB, NFL, NCAAF, NHL, MLB, UFC/MMA, major soccer). If verdict is SIT, include a "Closest Misses (Popular Games)" section with 3-6 near-miss examples, each with: matchup, market, de-vig implied %, true prob %, edge %, threshold gap, and plain-English sit reason. Keep obscure leagues in a short "Other Markets Checked" line unless they contain the top edge of the slate. Closest-miss selection rule: include only near-positive edges in the range +0.5% to +1.9% (strictly below T3). Exclude all negative-edge examples from Closest Misses. If no markets fall in +0.5% to +1.9%, output: "No near-miss positive edges in the +0.5% to +1.9% window." Free-tier API guardrails: perform one full scan at 6:00 AM CT and avoid broad rescans in the same day unless explicitly requested by user. Reuse cached sport responses for 15 minutes (20 minutes for niche/low-liquidity sports). Prioritize popular leagues first in reporting. If remaining API quota is low (or likely low), stop broad expansion and continue with only highest-priority leagues; if verification cannot be completed safely under quota, output CANNOT_VERIFY_ODDS — SIT.
